articles.full_text:
Ahmedabad Others
Red alert issued as heavy rains set to lash Gujarat
Dang, Valsad, Tapi, Narmada among districts at risk; IMD warns of extremely heavy rainfall, waterlogging, thunderstorms till July 6
Heavy rains in Surat left the roads waterlogged
Ahmedabad Mirror
Jul 01, 2026 06:00 AM | UPDATED: Jul 01, 2026 01:57 PM | 5 min read
Gujarat is set to witness a rise in monsoon activity over the next seven days as the southwest monsoon moves further across the state. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued red, orange and yellow alerts for several districts, warning of heavy to extremely heavy rainfall between July 3 and July 6.
The worst impact is expected in South Gujarat, where several districts may receive intense showers. The IMD has issued a red alert for Dang, Valsad, Daman and Dadra & Nagar Haveli on July 3, predicting extremely heavy rainfall at isolated places. On July 4, the red alert will cover Dang, Tapi and Narmada.
An orange alert has been issued for Surat, Tapi, Navsari, Valsad, Narmada, Bharuch, Chhota Udepur and Vadodara between July 3 and July 6. Several parts of Saurashtra, including Amreli, Bhavnagar, Gir Somnath, Rajkot and Junagadh, are also likely to receive heavy showers.
The weather department has also issued a yellow alert for widespread thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds with speeds of 40-50 kmph across North, Central and South Gujarat. In Ahmedabad, the maximum temperature on Tuesday was recorded at 39.6°C, while high humidity levels added to the discomfort. The city witnessed cloudy skies during the day. Rainfall activity is expected to reach Ahmedabad from Thursday, according to IMD.

Gujarat receives widespread monsoon relief after a dry June, with IMD issuing a heavy rainfall Orange Alert for several districts. Vapi recorded 5.5 inches in six hours, causing inundation but bringing much-needed relief to farmers. Multiple weather systems are set to intensify rainfall across the state, narrowing the 82% June deficit.

Summarized by AI; it may make mistakes. Check important info
Vapi Receives 5.5 Inches in Six Hours
After recording its driest June in years, the southwest monsoon has finally become active across Gujarat, bringing widespread rainfall and much-needed relief across the state. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heavy to very heavy rainfall alert for the next five days, with several districts under an Orange Alert.
Vapi Records 5.5 Inches in Six Hours
The heaviest rainfall was recorded in Valsad district, where Vapi received 5.5 inches of rain in just six hours, between 2 am and 8 am on Wednesday. Valsad town recorded 3.7 inches, Pardi 3.4 inches, and Umargam 2.4 inches during the same period.
The intense downpour inundated several low-lying areas in Vapi, including Gitanagar and Chala, while the city's old and new railway underpasses were flooded, disrupting traffic and affecting commuters during the morning rush. Despite the disruption, the rainfall brought much-needed relief to farmers, particularly those cultivating paddy, after a prolonged dry spell.
Across Gujarat, rain was recorded in 65 talukas, with Navsari receiving around 4 inches of rainfall.
Rainfall Reported Across Several Districts
Apart from Valsad district and Navsari, more than 1 inch of rainfall was recorded in several places, including Jalalpore and Chikhli (Navsari), Savarkundla (Amreli), Dharampur and Kaprada (Valsad), Surat city, Kamrej, Palsana, Bardoli, Olpad, Vaso (Kheda), Waghai (Dang), Una (Gir Somnath), and Valod (Tapi).
Four Weather Systems to Trigger Heavy Rain
According to the IMD, four major weather systems are expected to become active simultaneously, bringing together easterly and westerly winds that are likely to trigger widespread heavy rainfall.
These include:
An offshore trough extending from Maharashtra to north Kerala.
Another trough stretching from Madhya Pradesh to the Konkan-Goa region.
A western disturbance expected to affect northwest India from July 2.
A low-pressure area likely to form over the Bay of Bengal on July 3.
The interaction of these systems is expected to intensify rainfall across Gujarat over the coming days.
Orange Alert for Several Districts
The IMD has issued an Orange Alert for heavy to very heavy rainfall in:
July 1–3: Surat, Dang, Tapi, Navsari, Valsad, Daman and Dadra & Nagar Haveli.
July 4–5: Chhota Udepur, Bharuch, Surat, Navsari, Valsad, Amreli and Bhavnagar.
The weather department has also warned of strong winds over the next five days and advised local authorities to remain on alert.
June Ends With 82% Rainfall Deficit
Gujarat received only 19.9 mm of rainfall in June, compared to the normal average of 110.8 mm, resulting in an 82% rainfall deficit. However, with the onset of July, the monsoon has intensified, raising hopes that the rainfall shortfall will narrow in the coming days.
